frontmatter.title: Weak algebra for a monad

mainmatter.p: We shall refer to an algebra for the underlying endofunctor of a monad as a weak algebra, following Joyal and Moerdijk.

frontmatter.title: Algebras for endofunctors and monads
Algebra for an endofunctor

mainmatter.p: Let F be an endofunctor on a category \mathcal {C}; an algebra for F is defined to be an object X \in \mathcal {C} equipped with a mapping { F X } \xrightarrow {{ \alpha _{ X } }}{ X }.

frontmatter.title: Homomorphism of algebras

mainmatter.p: Let F be an endofunctor on a category \mathcal {C} and let X , Y be a pair of algebras for F; a homomorphism of algebras from X to Y is given by a morphism { X } \xrightarrow {{ h }}{ Y } in \mathcal {C} that preserves the algebra structure in the following sense:

frontmatter.title: Restricting endofunctor algebras along endomorphisms

mainmatter.p: Let X be a an algebra for an endofunctor { \mathcal {C} } \xrightarrow {{ F }}{ \mathcal {C} }, and let { X } \xrightarrow {{ x }}{ X } be an endomorphism in \mathcal {C}. We define the restriction of X by { X } \xrightarrow {{ x }}{ X } to be the algebra x \cdot X whose carrier is X with { F X } \xrightarrow {{ \alpha _{ x \cdot X } }}{ X } defined to be the following composite:

frontmatter.title: Algebra for a monad

mainmatter.p: Let \mathbb {T} \equiv { \mathopen {} \left ( T, \eta , \mu \right ) \mathclose {}} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C}. An algebra for \mathbb {T} is defined to be a T-algebra X satisfying the following unit and associativity laws:

frontmatter.title: The induced monad algebra on a fixed point
The induced monad algebra

mainmatter.p: Let \mathbb {T} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C}, and let X be a weak algebra for \mathbb {T} that is a fixed point, i.e. the algebra map is invertible by { X } \xrightarrow {{ \pi _{ X } }}{ T X }. We may define the induced \mathbb {T}-algebra \bar { X } to have the same carrier object as X and with the algebra map { T X } \xrightarrow {{ \alpha _{ \bar { X } } }}{ X } defined as follows:
We must show that our construction in fact gives rise to a monad algebra.

frontmatter.title: Unitality of the induced monad algebra

mainmatter.p: Let \mathbb {T} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C}, and let X be a weak algebra for \mathbb {T} for which the algebra map is invertible by { X } \xrightarrow {{ \pi _{ X } }}{ T X }. Then the induced monad algebra \bar { X } is unital.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: We are trying to check that the following morphism is an identity:

ol.li: We unfold the definition of the algebra map \alpha _{ \bar { X } }.
We use the unit law for \mathbb {T}.
Finally, we use the fact that \pi _{ X } is inverse to \alpha _{ X }.

frontmatter.title: Associativity of the induced monad algebra

mainmatter.p: Let \mathbb {T} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C}, and let X be a weak algebra for \mathbb {T} for which the algebra map is invertible by { X } \xrightarrow {{ \pi _{ X } }}{ T X }. Then the induced monad algebra \bar { X } is associative.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: We are trying to check that the following identity holds:

ol.li: We will start on the right-hand side. We first unfold the definition of the algebra map.
We use the fact that \pi _{ X } inverts \alpha _{ X }.
We use the associativity of the monad \mathbb {T}.
Folding the definition of the algebra map, we have the desired cell.

frontmatter.title: The induced monad algebra is a monad algebra

mainmatter.p: Let \mathbb {T} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C}, and let X be a weak algebra for \mathbb {T} for which the algebra map is invertible. Then the induced monad algebra \bar { X } is in fact a monad algebra.
mainmatter.block: Proof. Because it is unital and associative.

frontmatter.title: Lifting homomorphisms to the induced monad algebra

mainmatter.p: Let \mathbb {T} \equiv { \mathopen {} \left ( T , \eta , \mu \right ) \mathclose {}} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C} and let I be an invertible weak algebra for \mathbb {T}; let X be an arbitrary \mathbb {T}-algebra equipped with an endomap { X } \xrightarrow {{ x }}{ X } \in \mathcal {C}. Then a homomorphism { I } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ x \cdot X } also tracks a homomorphism { \bar { I } } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ X }, where \bar { I } is the induced monad algebra of I and x \cdot X is the restriction of X along x.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: Suppose that { I } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ x \cdot X } is a homomorphism; we must check that the same map tracks a homomorphism { \bar { I } } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ X }.

ol.li: First, we unfold the definition of the induced monad algebra.
Next, we use the algebra homomorphism law for { I } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ x \cdot X }.
We unfold the restricted algebra map.
Then we use the associativity law for the algebra X.
We use the algebra homomorphism law for { I } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ x \cdot X } again.
We recall that \pi _{ I } and \alpha _{ I } are inverse.

frontmatter.title: Algebra for an endofunctor

mainmatter.p: Let F be an endofunctor on a category \mathcal {C}; an algebra for F is defined to be an object X \in \mathcal {C} equipped with a mapping { F X } \xrightarrow {{ \alpha _{ X } }}{ X }.

frontmatter.title: From successor algebra homomorphisms to weak algebra homomorphisms

mainmatter.p: Let \mathbb {T} \equiv { \mathopen {} \left ( T , \eta , \mu \right ) \mathclose {}} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C} and let I be an invertible weak algebra for \mathbb {T}; let X be an arbitrary successor algebra. Then a successor algebra homomorphism { \bar { I } } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ X } also tracks a weak algebra homomorphism { I } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ s \cdot X }, where \bar { I } is the induced successor algebra of I and s \cdot X is the successor restriction of X.

ol.li: First, we unfold the algebra structure of the successor restriction.
We then use the successor algebra homomorphism property of { \bar { I } } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ X }.
Next we use the algebra homomorphism property of { \bar { I } } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ X }.
We unfold the definition of the induced monad algebra.
We unfold the successor map on the induced successor algebra.
We contract the composition of the initial (weak) algebra map with its inverse.
Finally, we use the unit law of the monad \mathbb {T}.

frontmatter.title: From initial weak algebras to initial successor algebras

mainmatter.p: Let I be the initial weak algebra for a monad \mathbb {T} on a category \mathcal {C}. Then the induced successor algebra \bar { I } is the initial successor algebra for \mathbb {T}.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: Let X be a successor algebra; we have shown that weak algebra homomorphism { I } \xrightarrow {{ \iota }}{ s \cdot X } from I to the successor restriction of X also tracks a successor algebra homomorphism { \bar { I } } \xrightarrow {{ \iota }}{ X }. To see that this homomorphism is unique, fix any successor algebra homomorphism { \bar { I } } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ X }; we have also shown that such a homomorphism also tracks a (weak algebra) homomorphism { I } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ s \cdot X }. But { I } \xrightarrow {{ \iota }}{ s \cdot X } is the such weak algebra homomorphism, so we have \iota = j.

frontmatter.title: Weak algebra homomorphisms and successor algebra homomorphisms are interchangeable

mainmatter.p: Let \mathbb {T} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C} and let I be an invertible weak algebra for \mathbb {T}; let X be an arbitrary successor algebra. Then a morphism { I } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ X } in \mathcal {C} tracks a weak algebra homomorphism { I } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ s \cdot X } if and only if it tracks a successor algebra homomorphism { \bar { I } } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ X }, where \bar { I } is the induced successor algebra of I and s \cdot X is the successor restriction of X.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: This follows from the following two lemmas:

ol.li: lifting homomorphisms to the induced successor algebra;
from successor algebra homomorphisms to weak algebra homomorphisms.

frontmatter.title: Lifting homomorphisms to the induced successor algebra

mainmatter.p: Let \mathbb {T} \equiv { \mathopen {} \left ( T , \eta , \mu \right ) \mathclose {}} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C} and let I be an invertible weak algebra for \mathbb {T}; let X be an arbitrary successor algebra. Then a homomorphism { I } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ s \cdot X } also tracks a successor algebra homomorphism { \bar { I } } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ X }, where \bar { I } is the induced successor algebra of I and s \cdot X is the successor restriction of X.
mainmatter.block:
Proof. We have already seen that { I } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ s \cdot X } tracks an algebra homomorphism { \bar { I } } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ X }. It remains to show that this homomorphism preserves the successor operation.
We first unfold the successor operation of the induced successor algebra on I.
Then we use the algebra property of { I } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ s \cdot X }.
Then we unfold the restricted algebra structure.
Finally we the unitality of the monad algebra X.

frontmatter.title: The induced successor algebra on a fixed point

mainmatter.p: Let \mathbb {T} \equiv { \mathopen {} \left ( T , \eta , \mu \right ) \mathclose {}} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C}, and let X be a weak algebra for \mathbb {T}. The monad algebra \bar { X } induced by X can be equipped with the structure of a successor algebra by defining { X } \xrightarrow {{ s _{ \bar { X } } }}{ X } to be the following map:

frontmatter.title: Lifting homomorphisms to the induced monad algebra

mainmatter.p: Let \mathbb {T} \equiv { \mathopen {} \left ( T , \eta , \mu \right ) \mathclose {}} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C} and let I be an invertible weak algebra for \mathbb {T}; let X be an arbitrary \mathbb {T}-algebra equipped with an endomap { X } \xrightarrow {{ x }}{ X } \in \mathcal {C}. Then a homomorphism { I } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ x \cdot X } also tracks a homomorphism { \bar { I } } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ X }, where \bar { I } is the induced monad algebra of I and x \cdot X is the restriction of X along x.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: Suppose that { I } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ x \cdot X } is a homomorphism; we must check that the same map tracks a homomorphism { \bar { I } } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ X }.

ol.li: First, we unfold the definition of the induced monad algebra.
Next, we use the algebra homomorphism law for { I } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ x \cdot X }.
We unfold the restricted algebra map.
Then we use the associativity law for the algebra X.
We use the algebra homomorphism law for { I } \xrightarrow {{ j }}{ x \cdot X } again.
We recall that \pi _{ I } and \alpha _{ I } are inverse.

frontmatter.title: The induced monad algebra is a monad algebra

mainmatter.p: Let \mathbb {T} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C}, and let X be a weak algebra for \mathbb {T} for which the algebra map is invertible. Then the induced monad algebra \bar { X } is in fact a monad algebra.
mainmatter.block: Proof. Because it is unital and associative.

frontmatter.title: Associativity of the induced monad algebra

mainmatter.p: Let \mathbb {T} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C}, and let X be a weak algebra for \mathbb {T} for which the algebra map is invertible by { X } \xrightarrow {{ \pi _{ X } }}{ T X }. Then the induced monad algebra \bar { X } is associative.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: We are trying to check that the following identity holds:

ol.li: We will start on the right-hand side. We first unfold the definition of the algebra map.
We use the fact that \pi _{ X } inverts \alpha _{ X }.
We use the associativity of the monad \mathbb {T}.
Folding the definition of the algebra map, we have the desired cell.

frontmatter.title: The induced monad algebra

mainmatter.p: Let \mathbb {T} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C}, and let X be a weak algebra for \mathbb {T} that is a fixed point, i.e. the algebra map is invertible by { X } \xrightarrow {{ \pi _{ X } }}{ T X }. We may define the induced \mathbb {T}-algebra \bar { X } to have the same carrier object as X and with the algebra map { T X } \xrightarrow {{ \alpha _{ \bar { X } } }}{ X } defined as follows:

frontmatter.title: The successor restriction of a successor algebra

mainmatter.p: Let \mathbb {T} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C}, and let X be an successor algebra for \mathbb {T}. We define the successor restriction of X to be the weak algebra s \cdot X : \equiv s _{ X } \cdot X defined by restricting X along its successor map.

frontmatter.title: Unitality of the induced monad algebra

mainmatter.p: Let \mathbb {T} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C}, and let X be a weak algebra for \mathbb {T} for which the algebra map is invertible by { X } \xrightarrow {{ \pi _{ X } }}{ T X }. Then the induced monad algebra \bar { X } is unital.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: We are trying to check that the following morphism is an identity:

ol.li: We unfold the definition of the algebra map \alpha _{ \bar { X } }.
We use the unit law for \mathbb {T}.
Finally, we use the fact that \pi _{ X } is inverse to \alpha _{ X }.

frontmatter.title: Algebraic set theory
frontmatter.meta: @book{joyal-moerdijk-1995, author = {Joyal, Andr\'{e} and Moerdijk, Ieke}, publisher = {Cambridge University Press}, year = {1995}, doi = {10.1017/CBO9780511752483}, series = {London Mathematical Society Lecture Note Series}, title = {Algebraic Set Theory}, }
